description: Expected layout for structural_disturbance: D_F2 is a RAM-style disturbance on the endogenous factor F2. Because F2 is non-terminal (it loads on X1/X2/X3), D_F2 gets placement='beside' and shares rank 1 with F2. F1 is exogenous at rank 2, indicators at rank 0.
notes: D_F2 satisfies all four error-node detection conditions (one free self-loop, no incoming paths, one fixed-1.0 outgoing path to F2) but its target F2 is non-terminal, so placement is 'beside' rather than 'below'. D_F2 must share rank 1 and the same y-coordinate as F2, placed to its right.
